description_long: This object consists of two round bowls, one larger and one smaller, both with a slightly flared rim and a faceted, polygonal exterior. The bowls have a dark, reddish-brown coloration with subtle variations and mottling, suggesting a ceramic or earthenware material. The surfaces appear matte with a slight roughness, lacking any visible gloss or shine. Both bowls are empty and show no visible decorative patterns or ornamentation. The edges are slightly irregular, giving a hand-crafted appearance. There are no visible handles, lids, or additional features beyond the basic bowl form.
description: A pair of dark, reddish-brown, faceted bowls with a matte, slightly rough finish and no visible decoration.
description_view_0: View 0 shows both bowls from above and slightly to the side, clearly displaying their open interiors and the size difference between the two.
description_view_1: View 1 presents the bowls from a lower angle, emphasizing the exterior faceting and the relative heights of the bowls.
description_view_2: View 2 focuses more on the larger bowl, with the smaller bowl partially obscured behind it, highlighting the depth and rim shape.
description_view_3: View 3 shows the bowls from the opposite side, with the larger bowl in the foreground and the smaller bowl behind, emphasizing their relative positions and the polygonal exterior.
